news.title: Debitam - Online Account Filing
news.summary:
Debitam, as a word in Latin, translates into English as “due”.
What we started as Online Account Filing 5 years ago, we rebranded ourselves as Debitam last year.
Being a unique and strongly tied term with the nature of our company filing service, we choose Debitam as our new name as a reminder of the fact that we will never let your company filings go overdue.
We pride ourselves with our meticulous tax filing reminding service and with protecting you from paying late filing penalties to Companies House and HMRC in the last 5 years, every day.
Meet the team, find out more about DEBITEM, give us a call, or just go on our website to read our blog.

news.title: Online Account Filing Revenue, Funding & Valuation
news.summary:
About Debitam - Online Account Filing Online Accounting Service | Bookkeeping | Payroll | Personal Tax Return Filing to HMRC | Corporation Tax Filing | HMRC ONLINE FILING: Our aim is to help small business owners` tax return and account filing compliance works by using cutting-edge technology and adopting a versatile corporate culture.

SERVICES: - Company Accounts & Tax Return - Self-Assessment Tax Return - VAT & Bookkeeping services, by adjusting ourselves with your previous accounting application.

news.title: SA1 Form Guide: Register for Self Assessment
news.summary:
This guide will walk you through everything you need to know about the SA1 form—who it’s for, why it matters, and how to complete it with ease.
The SA1 form is an official document from HMRC that allows you to register for Self Assessment if you're not automatically enrolled.
For more information about rental income property tax, read our blog Short Term Property Income Lettings.
To delve into a recently popular topic, foreign income and its tax implications, read SA106: Your Guide to Foreign Income Tax.
Once you've submitted your SA1 form, HMRC typically issues a response within 21 days, though this timeframe may be longer during peak periods.

news.title: The Documents You Need to Start a Business
news.summary:
In this article, we give a simplified breakdown of the legally required documents to start your business.
These include your Articles of Association, shareholders agreement, directors' service agreement, statement of work, share options agreement and non-disclosure agreements.
Shareholders Agreement Your shareholders' agreement sets out the relationship between the shareholders of the company.
Non-Disclosure Agreement The non-disclosure agreement (NDA) is used to protect confidential information.
Having these key documents in place will help to ensure that your business is run smoothly and efficiently from the start.

news.title: Formation of a company - Beginners Guide
news.summary:
Does it help to build up a picture of the intensity of company formation in the UK?
If you think limited company formation works well for your business here is a guide to the steps involved in new company formation.
Also, the name needs to be appropriately included in all the company’s documents, publicity, letters, website, etc.
The director of a company is responsible for running and carrying out the administration tasks of the company.
To be a director of the company, the following conditions need to be met: The person should be over 16 years of age Should not be disqualified to be a director Company Secretary A public limited company needs to appoint a company secretary while private companies have no such obligation by law.

news.title: Online Account Filing Service for Businesses and Companies
news.summary:
How are you different from a high street accountant?
Traditionally we like high street accountants for their proximity, mutual trust and the personal touch.
You will have a dedicated account manager assigned as your point of contact for all services that is just a phone call away.
In comparison to high street firms, you will find us tremendously lower for exactly the same services.
We even offer 30 minutes of absolutely free initial advice (no hard sell, we promise, no one likes that).
